reforms.description: The Dutch government has introduced fiscal measures to promote research and development, including tax reductions on labor costs for R&D staff and streamlined patenting processes to encourage firms to seek patent protection for their innovations. Additionally, institutional and tax reforms are proposed to encourage financial companies to provide risk capital and support the commercialization of private research.
reforms.component_name: R&D Tax Incentives and Patent Streamlining
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are designed to enhance R&D investment and facilitate the commercialization of innovations, which are expected to boost productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into effect as part of the government's ongoing efforts to enhance R&D and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in the fiscal and regulatory environment for R&D and innovation, but do not constitute a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The government has proposed to introduce institutional and tax reforms in order to give financial companies both the scope for action and the incentive to play a more dynamic role in the Dutch economy – i.e. to provide risk capital and support the commercialisation of private research.

reforms.description: The Dutch government has concentrated its agencies providing first-line advice to start-ups into 'Enterprise Houses', which will be implemented nationally to enhance service delivery at the local level.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to advice and support for start-ups, this initiative is expected to foster entrepreneurship and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced and is set to be implemented nationally.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant improvement in the support structure for start-ups, although it is not a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The concentration of government agencies providing first-line advice to start-ups into 'Enterprise Houses', to be applied nationally from the beginning of 1998, should also provide better delivery of these services at the local level.

reforms.description: The Technology Rating Foundation, with assistance from Dutch authorities, has developed a technology certification scheme aimed at reducing the costs associated with evaluating high-tech projects by providing expert evaluations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to lower barriers for high-tech projects, facilitating innovation and potentially leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been developed and announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a technology certification scheme represents a significant step in improving the evaluation process for high-tech projects.
reforms.source_quote: One response, developed by the Technology Rating Foundation with assistance from the Dutch authorities, is the technology certification scheme.

reforms.description: The Dutch government has initiated efforts to improve linkages between academic research and the business sector, including new incentive structures to ensure research is more demand-driven.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing collaboration between academia and industry, this initiative aims to drive innovation and economic growth through more relevant research outputs.
reforms.status_evidence: The efforts to improve linkages have been put into effect as part of the government's innovation strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to strengthen the connection between research and market needs.
reforms.source_quote: Further efforts to find ways of commercialising the results of academic work seem warranted.

reforms.description: The Dutch government initiated the WBSO (Act to promote R&D), which provides tax reductions on labor costs for R&D staff, aimed at increasing investment in R&D among small businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This tax incentive is designed to stimulate R&D investment among small businesses, which is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The WBSO has been successfully implemented and is actively promoting R&D investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The WBSO represents a major reform in the funding landscape for R&D, significantly altering the incentives for small businesses.
reforms.source_quote: The WBSO (Act to promote R&D) grants a tax reduction on labour cost of R&D staff.

reforms.description: The Dutch government is expanding its cluster policy to promote technological cooperation and the emergence of innovative clusters and networks of businesses and research institutes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering technological cooperation and innovation clusters, this policy aims to enhance collaboration and drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The expansion of the cluster policy has been put into effect as part of the government's innovation strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of cluster policy represents a significant effort to enhance innovation ecosystems.
reforms.source_quote: The emphasis in technology policy has shifted to promoting technological co-operation and the emergence of innovative clusters and networks.
